title: [Hooks] Stop hook never fires after `run:create --harness claude-code` — first iteration is never triggered, run parked at `state=created`
summary: A Claude Code harness-bound run can be created and have its session state written successfully, but the host Stop hook is not invoked after the run:create assistant turn. The run remains at state=created with only RUN_CREATED and no pending effects until a manual run:iterate generates the first EFFECT_REQUESTED.
rootCauseHypothesis: The first iteration is a chicken-and-egg gap: stop-hook continuation relies on the host invoking the Stop hook, but immediately after run:create there are no pending effects and no first run:iterate has occurred. Existing stop-hook code can block a created run if invoked, but the reported failure is that the host never invokes it after run:create.
preferredFixDirection: Favor making run:create --harness claude-code seed the first iteration for eligible hook-driven, session-bound, non-bare runs before returning, so the run is delivered at waiting/completed/failed rather than unreachable created. The design task must still compare this against a stop-hook created-state special case and document the tradeoff.
acceptanceFocus: `babysitter run:create --harness claude-code` with a valid process and successful session binding must not leave the run with only RUN_CREATED and zero pending effects.
The newly created hook-driven run should reach the first orchestration result without requiring a manual `run:iterate` or user nudge.
The fix must not double-iterate when a harness or caller already performed the first iteration.
Bare runs must remain created and uniterated until a process is assigned.
Failed or unresolved session binding must not hide errors by auto-iterating.
Existing stop-hook continuation, #158/#159 waiting behavior, and #166 missing-session recovery semantics must remain intact.
CLI JSON and human output should expose any initial iteration result clearly enough for callers and tests to reason about it.
nonGoals: Do not rewrite the Claude Code hook installation flow unless runtime tracing proves it is required.
Do not broaden the change to unrelated harnesses without an explicit compatibility reason.
Do not change bare-run assignment semantics.
Do not weaken stop-hook backoff, breakpoint waiting, completion-proof handling, or missing-session recovery behavior.
Do not introduce shell-oriented process subtasks in this repository process.
qualityGates: Regression tests fail before implementation and pass after implementation.
Targeted CLI/harness tests prove run:create --harness claude-code reaches the first EFFECT_REQUESTED for a process with a first task.
Negative tests prove bare runs and session binding errors do not auto-iterate.
SDK test suite passes or any pre-existing unrelated failures are documented with command output.
Compatibility review approves interactions with #158, #159, and #166 hook behavior.
plannedPhases: Read issue #170, comments, labels, and related issue boundaries.
Trace run:create, createRun, bindSession, stop-hook run-state resolution, and run:iterate paths.
Design first-iteration wakeup semantics and use a sparse breakpoint only if a maintainer decision is genuinely required.
Author failing regression tests before implementation.
Implement a focused fix with up to three verification/review attempts.
Run targeted and full SDK verification gates.
Perform compatibility review and final acceptance.
